footer.tagline: Self-healing browser QA — describe a test in plain English; Scout verifies it and replays it for free.
home.title: Delegate the build. Keep the outcome in focus.
home.intro: Code got cheap — agents write it on demand. What stays scarce is judgment: does the product do what you pictured? Scout is the gate that proves it, from one plain-language sentence, and re-checks itself when the UI moves.
home.subEmphasis: No test code to own, no human in the loop.
home.verdictLegendLabel: Verdict ramp — the outcomes Scout reports
home.instrumentAlt: A calibrated optical instrument bringing the product into focus, with a verdict reading of verified.
manifesto.beforeBody: Shipping a feature meant weeks of engineering. Quality rode along with the people who wrote it.
manifesto.nowBody: Agents produce it on demand. The scarce thing is judgment — does the outcome match what you pictured?
manifesto.withScoutBody: State the expectation once, in plain language. Scout becomes the gate that proves the product still clears it — unattended.
feature.lede: An agent confirms your intent in a real browser, then Scout records the path and replays it deterministically. When the UI drifts and that path breaks, Scout re-aims itself — re-verifies, re-records, hands you a partial to sign off. No human babysitting the suite. That unattended heal is what makes it safe to point at code an agent just wrote.
feature.step01Body: One sentence in plain English. No selectors, no waits, no fixtures to wire up by hand.
feature.step02Body: The agent drives a real browser, confirms the intent holds, and records exactly what it did.
feature.step03Title: It locks in a deterministic script
feature.step03Body: From then on, runs are pure replay — fast, free, and identical. No model in the loop.
feature.step04Body: When the script breaks because the page changed, the agent returns, re-verifies the intent, re-records, and hands you a partial to sign off. The loop closes back to 01.
feature.terminalCaption: A deterministic replay reading — no model in the loop.
verdict.lede: Every run resolves to a single verdict, each mapped to a stable exit code — the contract for scout report --check and dashboards.
verdict.verifiedMeaning: The flow held. A deterministic script was recorded or replayed.
verdict.partialMeaning: Scout healed a broken step on its own — review the new path before you trust it.
verdict.failedMeaning: A step no longer holds and could not be healed. The diff tells you which one.
verdict.blockedMeaning: Scout never got to observe — the app wouldn't start or the page wouldn't load.
docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Guides and reference for running Scout.
docs.searchUnavailable: Search runs on the built site — try the production build.
lab.title: The Lab
lab.description: Every Scout feature, live in the browser — a recorded demo of it working, the surface you can exercise yourself, and the exact .scout.md scenario that runs against this page.
lab.h1Focus: and the spec that proves it
lab.intro: Each card below shows Scout verifying the feature in a short recorded demo, a working surface you can exercise yourself, and the exact .scout.md scenario behind it. The spec on the page is the spec that runs — one file, no drift.
lab.ciTitle: Scout runs these very cases in CI
lab.ciNote: On every push and pull request, GitHub Actions builds this Lab fresh and replays the exact .scout.md scenarios below against it — deterministically, with no AI. Open the latest run to see Scout verify each one.
lab.ciBadgeAlt: Scout workflow status on GitHub Actions
lab.ciCta: View the latest Scout run on GitHub
lab.demoCaption: Recorded with scout --demo-video. Press play to watch the scenario run.
lab.multiTabBtn: Open the report in a new tab
lab.multiTabHint: Opens /lab/report/ in a new tab — the agent follows the switch and asserts on the new tab.
lab.couponHint: Scout types $ENV:LAB_COUPON here and asserts the coupon was accepted — the literal value never enters the spec, the recorded script, or the model.
lab.networkError: The order API did not respond cleanly.
lab.networkHint: Fires GET /lab/api/order.json — Scout asserts 2xx with an orderId.
lab.consoleIdle: Open your browser console, then click.
lab.consoleHint: Scout asserts a console log containing DIAG:[scout-lab] appeared.
lab.authHint: Scout captures this session once with scout login, then replays the qa profile.
lab.quickEyebrow: Point your own Scout at it
lab.quickBody: This page is a stable target. Point Scout at it with one npx command — no install — and watch it verify a scenario against a site you did not write. The fastest way to see a first reading.
lab.reportBody: This tab was opened from the Lab. Scout followed the click here and runs its assertions on this tab.
notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: This page does not exist or has been moved.
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
